name: angular
description: Modern Angular (v20+) expert with deep knowledge of Signals, Standalone Components, Zoneless applications, SSR/Hydration, and reactive patterns.
capabilities: Configure Zoneless Angular applications for better performance
Set up server-side rendering with hydration
Build functional route guards and resolvers
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analysis findings were false positives. The skill contains documentation-only content (markdown and JSON configuration) with no executable code. URL references point to official Angular documentation. Markdown code formatting was incorrectly flagged as shell execution. No actual security risks detected.
valueStatement: Struggling with outdated Angular patterns and complex NgModules? This skill provides expert guidance on modern Angular (v20+) with Signals, Standalone Components, and Zoneless architecture.
userTitle: Build Modern Angular Applications with Signals
limitations: Does not cover AngularJS (1.x) migration - use angular-migration skill instead
Focuses on Angular v20+ patterns, not legacy NgModule architecture
Does not replace TypeScript expertise for general TypeScript issues

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers starting new Angular projects
useCases.title: New Angular Project Setup
useCases.description: Get expert guidance on bootstrapping a modern Angular application with Signals, Standalone Components, and optimal configuration from the start.

useCases.title: Legacy Angular Modernization
useCases.description: Plan and execute gradual migration from NgModules to Standalone Components, adopting Signals and Zoneless patterns incrementally.

useCases.title: Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Implement advanced patterns like deferred loading, incremental hydration, and OnPush change detection for maximum performance.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: Create a Signal-Based Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Angular component using Signals for state management. The component should display a counter with increment and decrement buttons, using signal inputs for initial value and signal outputs for events.

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Convert NgModule to Standalone
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me convert this existing NgModule-based Angular component to a Standalone Component. Update all imports, remove module dependencies, and ensure the component uses modern signal-based inputs and outputs.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Implement Zoneless Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through converting an existing Angular application to Zoneless mode. Explain the configuration changes needed, how to handle change detection triggers, and what patterns to use for async operations without zone.js.

promptTemplates.title: Expert: SSR with Incremental Hydration
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up server-side rendering with incremental hydration for an Angular application. Configure defer blocks with appropriate triggers (on viewport, on interaction, on idle) for optimal Core Web Vitals scores.

bestPractices: Use Signals for local component state and RxJS for async streams like HTTP requests
Enable OnPush change detection and let Signals trigger updates automatically
Prefer Standalone Components with direct imports over SharedModules for better tree-shaking
antiPatterns: Using RxJS BehaviorSubject for simple local state when Signals are sufficient
Loading entire feature modules eagerly instead of using loadComponent and defer blocks
Mixing legacy decorator patterns with Signals causing inconsistent change detection

faq.question: What Angular version does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Angular v20 and above, covering Signals (stable), Standalone Components (default), Zoneless mode, and modern SSR with hydration patterns.

faq.question: Should I use Signals or RxJS for state management?
faq.answer: Use Signals for local component state and derived values. Use RxJS for HTTP requests, event streams, and complex async flows. They complement each other with toSignal() and toObservable() interop.

faq.question: Is Zoneless mode production-ready?
faq.answer: Yes, Zoneless is stable in Angular v20+. It provides better performance and debugging. However, test thoroughly as some third-party libraries may still depend on zone.js.

faq.question: Do I need to rewrite my entire app to use Standalone Components?
faq.answer: No. Standalone Components can coexist with NgModule-based components. Migrate gradually, starting with new components and leaf components, then move up the component tree.

faq.question: What is the benefit of SSR with incremental hydration?
faq.answer: SSR provides fast initial page load and SEO benefits. Incremental hydration loads interactive JavaScript progressively, improving Time to Interactive and reducing main thread blocking.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for AngularJS (1.x) projects?
faq.answer: No. This skill covers modern Angular (v20+). For AngularJS migration, use the dedicated angular-migration skill which handles the significant architectural differences.
